title: all_users() full table scan on every client connect — scale concern
description: relay/src/ws.rs calls store.all_users() on every authenticated connection to build the DirectoryList. The query is SELECT pub_id,nickname FROM users ORDER BY first_seen ASC — a full table scan plus sort. At a few hundred users this is invisible (<1ms). At tens of thousands of persistent users the connect latency grows and the allocation per connection becomes non-trivial. Impact at scale: new connections slow down under load, creating a feedback loop where slow connects increase the backlog. Fix when user count crosses a few thousand: cache the directory in AppState and invalidate on write, or paginate DirectoryList.
